flashcards.definition: Underground layer of permeable rock or sediment that holds and transmits groundwater.
Massive US aquifer underlying the Great Plains; being depleted by irrigation faster than it recharges.
Pollution from a single identifiable source (e.g., factory pipe, sewage outfall).
Pollution from diffuse sources (e.g., agricultural runoff, urban stormwater).
Upper surface of the zone of saturation in an aquifer; drops when pumping exceeds recharge.
